label: ILC3
evidence: The atlas paper (Gopee et al., 2024; DOI: 10.1038/s41586-024-08002-x) identifies ILC3 explicitly in the context of prenatal skin. The paper states: 'lymphoid tissue inducer and ILC3 cells were also predicted to co-locate and interact with pre-Dc cells through ligand-receptor signals implicated in the regulation of cellular adhesion and migration (CXCL12-CXCR4 and CXCL12-DPP4).' Supplementary materials describe ILC3 as a distinct prenatal immune population co-locating with pre-dermal condensate cells based on correlation analyses.
